rationale_overall: Stand at 2022-12-31 close. Basket is AT cap=8 (MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA, ISRG, URA, RKLB, IONQ, LLY) and every slot maps cleanly to a named profile wave with at least one fresh Q4-2022 pre-date catalyst supporting hold; no swap candidate clears the bar of displacing an in-thesis name at the cap. Wave-by-wave review: (1) MSFT — AI/cloud infrastructure, Azure FY23 Q1 (reported 2022-10-25) grew 35% YoY in constant currency, Microsoft's hyperscaler AI capex thesis intact through the Q4 NASDAQ drawdown; buildup-phase pullback, not a crest. (2) GOOGL — search/AI platform, Q3 2022 reported 2022-10-25, the 2022-12-21 'code red' internal response to ChatGPT (released 2022-11-30) is a real competitive shot across the bow but is a 10-day-old news item against Google's own pre-existing AI stack (LaMDA, DeepMind, TPUs); financial impact is multi-year and ad/search cash flow remains the funding base for any AI response. Considered and rejected as a remove. (3) NVDA — Q3 FY23 reported 2022-11-16, Data Center revenue +31% YoY to $3.83B, H100 Hopper began shipping in the quarter; the August 2022 China A100/H100 export restriction is already in the price, the CHIPS Act (signed 2022-08-09) underwrites US data-center buildout, buildup intact. (4) ISRG — Q3 2022 reported 2022-10-18, da Vinci procedures +20% YoY, sole robotics slot, no pre-date thesis break. (5) URA — Cameco/Brookfield announced Westinghouse acquisition 2022-10-11 ($7.875B EV), uranium spot ended 2022 around $48/lb after rebounding from September lows, IRA (signed 2022-08-16) zero-emission nuclear PTC supports US fleet economics, EU REPowerEU + Japan restart pivot underwrites multi-year demand. (6) RKLB — sole rockets slot, Q3 reported 2022-11-09, Neutron development funded into 2023 and Electron cadence at 9 missions for the year (a company record); profile names rockets as a next wave, hold. (7) IONQ — sole quantum slot, IonQ Forte unveiled 2022 with early-access in 2022 and broader access targeted early 2023; no pre-date thesis break. (8) LLY — Q3 2022 reported 2022-11-01, Mounjaro received EU approval (2022-09-15), Canada approval (2022-11-22), and Australia approval (2022-12-19) all pre-date, reinforcing the GIP/GLP-1 demographics thesis; hold. Considered and rejected: adding LMT on Q3 2022 results (2022-10-18) and Ukraine HIMARS replenishment contracts (2022-10-19) — profile names rockets/robotics/quantum/nuclear as next waves, not defense primes; RKLB already holds the rockets slot and displacing any in-thesis name at cap=8 for a defense prime is off-profile. Considered and rejected: adding CCJ on the 2022-10-11 Westinghouse deal — URA already holds the nuclear/uranium slot and CCJ is a top-3 URA constituent, adding it would be concentration without diversification benefit. Considered and rejected: trimming GOOGL on 'code red' — single late-quarter news beat against an integrated AI/ads/cloud platform; not a thesis break, and removing GOOGL with eight days of new information would be reactive. Considered and rejected: trimming IONQ on Q4-2022 quantum-sector drawdown — sole quantum exposure in a named next-wave bucket, profile horizon is long, no pre-date catalyst break. Post-decision basket remains AT cap=8 and unchanged: MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA, ISRG, URA, RKLB, IONQ, LLY.
